# About

## Role
Python backend engineer with 3+ years of experience building scalable backend systems
and integrating AI capabilities into production applications.

## Current Position
Software Engineer at Openstream.ai (AI and Backend Systems), Bengaluru, India.
October 2022 – Present.

penstream.ai (AI and Backend Systems), Bengaluru, India.
October 2022 – Present. ## What I Work On
- High-concurrency Python APIs (FastAPI/Flask) sustaining sub-200ms response times
- LLM inference pipelines — prompt engineering, tool calling, structured output validation
- RAG pipelines and vector-based retrieval in production
- Real-time systems: WebRTC, Redis Pub/Sub, event-driven microservices
- Docker deployments on AWS and Azure

Redis Pub/Sub, event-driven microservices
- Docker deployments on AWS and Azure ## Philosophy
"I write backend services that disappear into the product — fast, quiet, and impossible
to break. I care about the boring fundamentals — clean architecture, code reviews,
SLOs that hold — because that's what lets the interesting parts ship."

## Based In
Bengaluru, India (IST / UTC+5:30)

## Availability
Actively looking for new opportunities. Open to remote roles and relocation.

# FAQ — Common Recruiter Questions

## Experience & Background

Q: How many years of experience do you have?
A: 3+ years as a Software Engineer at Openstream.ai (Oct 2022–Present), plus a
   5-year integrated AI/Robotics degree (2017–2022).

Q: What is your current company and role?
A: Software Engineer at Openstream.ai, working on AI and backend systems —
   LLM pipelines, real-time avatar infrastructure, and microservices.

systems —
   LLM pipelines, real-time avatar infrastructure, and microservices. Q: Are you from a product-based or service-based company?
A: Product-based. Openstream.ai builds AI-powered conversational avatar products.

Q: What level are you at — junior, mid, senior?
A: Mid-to-senior. With 3+ years owning end-to-end features, leading code reviews,
   and making architectural decisions in a product company, I operate at that level.

## Skills & Stack

ural decisions in a product company, I operate at that level.

## Skills & Stack Q: What is your strongest programming language?
A: Python. Used daily for FastAPI/Flask backends, LLM integration, data pipelines,
   and automation. I also work with TypeScript and JavaScript.

Q: Do you have LLM / AI experience?
A: Yes — it is a core part of my role. I've built LLM inference pipelines, RAG
   systems, prompt engineering frameworks, structured output validation, and
   tool-calling agents in production.

meworks, structured output validation, and
   tool-calling agents in production. Q: Do you have experience with RAG systems?
A: Extensively. Built RAG pipelines in production (40% hallucination reduction).
   Also built a hybrid RAG system (Qdrant + BM25 + RRF) as a public GitHub project.

Q: Do you know React / frontend?
A: My primary strength is backend, but I can work full-stack. I built this portfolio
   in React + TypeScript, and vibecheck has a full React frontend I shipped.

lio
   in React + TypeScript, and vibecheck has a full React frontend I shipped. Q: Do you have cloud/DevOps experience?
A: Yes — AWS (EC2, S3) and Microsoft Azure, Docker containerisation, and CI/CD
   pipelines. Maintained 99.9% uptime SLA on cloud-deployed services.

Q: Do you know vector databases?
A: Yes. I've worked with Qdrant (used in loan-RAG-assistant), and have experience
   with vector-based retrieval in production document extraction pipelines.

# Projects

## 1. VibeCheck — Real-time Emotion Detector (Featured)
Role: Full-stack | Year: 2025
Stack: React, TypeScript, FastAPI, MediaPipe, WebSockets, Groq LLM, Recharts
Live Demo: https://vibecheck-frontend-opal.vercel.app
GitHub: https://github.com/deepak20-singh/vibecheck_frontend

end-opal.vercel.app
GitHub: https://github.com/deepak20-singh/vibecheck_frontend Webcam-based real-time emotion detection and engagement scoring. Dual processing
modes: MediaPipe face landmarks (in-browser WASM, ~8fps, instant) and a Groq-powered
vision LLM (every 2 seconds, semantic depth). Live emotion timeline visualization.

sion LLM (every 2 seconds, semantic depth). Live emotion timeline visualization. Highlights:
- Dual-stream pipeline: MediaPipe 8fps + Groq LLM running in parallel
- Tracks 10 distinct emotions simultaneously with head pose detection (ZYX Euler)
- Privacy-first: video stays on-device, frames processed in-memory and discarded
- FastAPI backend with WebSocket sessions, IP-based rate limiting, daily quotas
- Zero persistent storage — all processing client-side or ephemeral

quotas
- Zero persistent storage — all processing client-side or ephemeral

--- ## 2. Insurance Document Extraction Platform (Flagship)
Role: Backend Engineer | Year: 2024–Present
Stack: Python, FastAPI, LLMs, Docker, AWS, Vector Search

FastAPI microservice that extracts structured JSON from insurance documents using
self-hosted LLMs, automating document understanding workflows for downstream systems.

hosted LLMs, automating document understanding workflows for downstream systems. Highlights:
- Multi-stage pipeline: conversion → classification → extraction → post-processing
- Supports PDF, Excel, and DOC inputs with modular, testable Python components
- Provider-agnostic LLM integration layer (sync/async, concurrency control)
- Vector-based search + prompt-driven extraction for accurate structured outputs
- Containerized on Docker, deployed to AWS

---

ole: Lead Backend | Year: 2023–Present

--- ## 4. Real-Time Session Management Dashboard
Role: Backend Engineer | Year: 2023
Stack: Flask, Redis, MongoDB, Microservices, CI/CD

Operator dashboard for live session telemetry. Redis-driven event bus,
RAG-grounded responses, role-based access control.

Highlights:
- Sub-100ms dashboard refresh via Redis caching + Pub/Sub
- RAG pipeline reduced hallucinations by 40%
- RBAC for access governance
- 25% drop in support tickets post-launch

--- ## 5. Health Monitoring Web Application
Role: Full-stack | Year: 2023
Stack: LLMs, RAG, Flask, Redis, AWS, Azure

Emotionally responsive medical dialogue system powered by an LLM avatar grounded
in domain data. Multi-cloud deployment across AWS EC2 and Azure App Services.

Highlights:
- 10k+ message exchanges/hour via modular APIs
- 35% faster event handling after Redis tuning
- P95 < 150ms across the inference path
- Multi-cloud: AWS EC2 + Azure App Services for high availability

--- ## 6. Loan RAG Assistant (GitHub)
Stack: Python, Qdrant, FastAPI, Redis, sentence-transformers, BM25, Groq (Llama-3)
GitHub: https://github.com/deepak20-singh/loan-RAG-assistant

RAG system for answering questions about small business loan policies. Uses hybrid
retrieval — vector search + BM25 + Reciprocal Rank Fusion — for more accurate
retrieval of lending-specific values (loan amounts, credit scores).

ore accurate
retrieval of lending-specific values (loan amounts, credit scores). Highlights:
- Hybrid retrieval: semantic (Qdrant) + keyword (BM25) + Reciprocal Rank Fusion
- sentence-transformers embeddings (BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5)
- Planned: Groq LLM integration, Pydantic structured outputs, Redis caching
